untested_candidates.name: l7_early_survival_gate
untested_candidates.description: At level 7, switch to a survival-first placement policy for the first 12 pieces: avoid creating holes and avoid exposing deep wells before any line-clear setup.

metadata.llm_notes: Level 7 has the weakest stability (avg_height=19.56, avg_holes=59.94), and failure episodes end after avg_pieces=1.0 with avg_holes=90.64. A strict early-game gate targets the immediate-collapse regime before scoring policy can matter.

untested_candidates.name: tetra_survive_tall_boards_hole_cap
untested_candidates.description: When height > 75%, keep conservative mode but add a hard hole cap: reject placements that increase hole count unless they clear lines immediately.

metadata.llm_notes: Parent already focuses on tall-board survival. Adding explicit hole-growth control is justified by the failure/success gap (avg_holes 90.64 vs 76.94, delta 13.7) while both operate near terminal height (~19), so hole trajectory is a stronger discriminator than height alone.

untested_candidates.name: outlier_entry_pattern_replay
untested_candidates.description: Learn and replay opening-to-midgame board patterns from high-surprise outlier episodes to increase the probability of entering the long-survival mode.

metadata.llm_notes: Novelty events at level 7 include 243, 206, and 164 lines (surprise up to 5.75), and top 1% outliers average 183.2 lines vs baseline 2.87. This suggests rare but valid trajectories exist; explicitly modeling their entry conditions should improve consistency.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_cap_12
untested_candidates.description: At level 7, enforce a strict opening policy for the first 12 pieces that rejects placements increasing holes unless they immediately clear a line.

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes collapse almost immediately (avg_pieces=1.0) with very high holes (avg_holes=90.64), while successful episodes sustain play with lower holes (76.94). A first-12-piece hole cap targets the early-collapse regime before score optimization matters.
metadata.enaction_note: Opening phase: double the hole penalty for pieces 0-12

untested_candidates.name: l7_bumpiness_guardrail
untested_candidates.description: On level 7, prioritize placements that keep local surface transitions smooth; avoid moves that sharply increase bumpiness unless they clear lines.

metadata.llm_notes: Bumpiness separates outcomes strongly (failure 3.09 vs success 1.38, delta 1.71). Height is similarly terminal in both groups (~19), so controlling surface volatility is a better discriminator than pure height minimization.
metadata.enaction_note: Always-on: 1.8x bumpiness penalty to keep surface smooth

untested_candidates.name: tetra_survive_tall_boards_hole_cap_opening_gate
untested_candidates.description: Keep the tall-board survival + hole-cap logic, but activate a stricter variant during the opening phase (first 12 pieces) to prevent immediate terminal trajectories.

metadata.llm_notes: Parent focuses on height>75% states, but current failures die at avg_pieces=1.0, indicating the key decision window is earlier. Novel L7 outliers (164-243 lines, surprise up to 5.75) imply viable trajectories exist if the opening avoids catastrophic early board damage.
metadata.enaction_note: Stricter variant: 2.5x hole penalty in opening phase

untested_candidates.name: l7_hole_collapse_gate_85
untested_candidates.description: On level-7 style states, switch immediately to survive mode when hole count exceeds collapse territory so the policy prioritizes stabilizing the board over greedy clears.

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44), with both near terminal height (~20), so a hole-triggered mode gate should separate collapse trajectories better than height-only control.

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake
untested_candidates.description: Apply a stronger global penalty to bumpiness so placements favor smoother surfaces and reduce the jagged stacks associated with immediate deaths.

metadata.llm_notes: Bumpiness is the strongest separator in this brief: failures are 7.92 vs successes 1.44 (delta 6.48), while height differs by only 0.41, indicating surface volatility control is high leverage.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_cap_20
untested_candidates.description: Extend the opening anti-hole window from the first 12 pieces to 20 pieces so early-board discipline persists through the fragile transition into midgame.

metadata.llm_notes: Failures still end very early (avg_pieces 23.23) with extreme holes (99.66), and success dissection shows outlier early holes are lower than baseline (78.8 vs 85.7), so extending the early hole cap should test whether stability needs a longer runway.

untested_candidates.name: l7_holes_collapse_gate_90
untested_candidates.description: Trigger survival mode when hole count exceeds 90 to prevent terminal collapse trajectories on wide-board runs.

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44), while height differs by only 0.41 (20.00 vs 19.59), so hole-threshold gating should separate collapse states earlier.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_20
untested_candidates.description: Extend strong hole suppression through the first 20 pieces to reduce early-board damage before midgame stabilization.

metadata.llm_notes: Failures end after only 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and success dissection shows lower outlier early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), so a longer opening discipline window is justified.

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_height_gate
untested_candidates.description: Keep bumpiness control but activate a stronger survive-mode gate once stack height enters the danger band.

metadata.llm_notes: Bumpiness is a large separator (7.92 failure vs 1.44 success), but level-7 average height is already high (19.63), so gating a survival switch at high height should focus anti-volatility behavior where it matters most.

untested_candidates.name: l7_hole_collapse_mode_gate_90
untested_candidates.description: Switch to survival strategy when hole count exceeds 90 so the policy prioritizes stabilization over greedy clears in collapse-prone states.

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44), while height differs by only 0.41 (20.00 vs 19.59), so a hole-triggered gate should separate collapse trajectories earlier.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_0_20
untested_candidates.description: Apply stronger hole suppression during the first 20 pieces to prevent early-board damage before midgame.

metadata.llm_notes: Failures end after only 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and success dissection shows lower outlier early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), supporting a longer opening discipline window.

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_height_gate_80
untested_candidates.description: Retain bumpiness control but intensify survival behavior once stack height enters the danger band.

metadata.llm_notes: Bumpiness is a major separator (7.92 failure vs 1.44 success, delta 6.48), and level-7 average height is already extreme (19.63), so adding a high-height gate should focus anti-volatility behavior at terminal risk.

untested_candidates.name: l7_hole_collapse_mode_gate_95
untested_candidates.description: Switch to survival strategy when holes exceed 95 so collapse-prone wide-board states stop prioritizing greedy clears.

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44) while height differs by only 0.41, so hole-triggered gating should separate terminal trajectories earlier.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_0_20_v2
untested_candidates.description: Apply stronger hole suppression through the first 20 pieces to prevent early-board damage before midgame stabilization.

metadata.llm_notes: Failures end after only 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and outlier episodes show lower early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), supporting a longer opening discipline window.

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_critical_height
untested_candidates.description: Increase bumpiness penalty only once the stack is in the critical height band, focusing anti-volatility control near terminal risk.

metadata.llm_notes: Bumpiness is a strong separator (7.92 failure vs 1.44 success, delta 6.48), and level-7 avg height is already 19.63, so a critical-height gate should concentrate the penalty where deaths cluster.

untested_candidates.name: l7_hole_collapse_mode_gate_85
untested_candidates.description: Switch to survive mode once hole count enters collapse territory so line-greedy behavior is suppressed in terminal-risk states.

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44), while max height differs by only 0.41 (20.00 vs 19.59), so hole-threshold gating should separate collapse trajectories earlier than height-only logic.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_0_24

metadata.llm_notes: Failures end after only 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and outlier success openings show lower early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), supporting a longer early discipline window.

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_critical_height_80
untested_candidates.description: Keep bumpiness control but intensify it only in critical-height states to avoid over-penalizing safe boards.

metadata.llm_notes: Bumpiness is the largest separator (7.92 failure vs 1.44 success, delta 6.48), and level-7 average height is already 19.63, so a high-height mode gate should focus anti-volatility behavior where deaths cluster.

untested_candidates.name: l7_hole_emergency_gate_85

metadata.llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44) while max height differs by only 0.41, so hole-triggered gating should separate terminal trajectories earlier than height-only control.

untested_candidates.name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_0_24_v2

metadata.llm_notes: Failures end after 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and outlier success openings have lower early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), supporting a stronger early discipline window.

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_height_critical
untested_candidates.description: Keep bumpiness suppression, but only intensify it once stack height is in the critical band so safe boards are not over-constrained.

untested_candidates.name: l7_hole_emergency_gate_80

untested_candidates.name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_height_gate_90

hypothesis_ledger.key_insight: Top hypothesis 'maximize_lines' wins 89% of the time after 50 evaluations.

failure_patterns.interpretation: Failures have avg height=20.0, holes=99.7, bumpiness=7.9. Games end after only 23 pieces.

success_patterns.interpretation: Successes have avg height=19.6, holes=75.2, 70 lines avg.

success_dissection.interpretation:
Top 1% episodes (5 games, avg 179 lines).
If outlier_early_holes << baseline_early_holes: tetra_holes_early_game is supported.
If outlier_mid_holes ≈ baseline_mid_holes but late_holes diverges: recovery hypothesis needed.

open_questions: Failures have 24.4 more holes on average — at what hole count does performance collapse?
12 hypotheses have been retired vs 9 active — what do the retired ones have in common? Is there a pattern to what fails?
45 candidate hypotheses have fewer than 10 evaluations — they need more evidence: ['maximize_gain_v1', 'minimize_v2', 'minimize_cost_v1', 'minimize_cost_v2', 'minimize_v4']
recommended_focus: LEVEL-SPECIFIC HYPOTHESES: Performance varies significantly by level. Consider hypotheses that specify WHEN they apply (e.g., 'at L7, prioritize flat boards').

inbox_schema.description: To update the hypothesis list, write a JSON array to experiments/tetra_inbox.json. The SlowLoop will ingest it at next nightly consolidation and archive the file.

supported_ops.note: Creates new hypothesis derived from parent; increments generation.

llm_priority_values.explore: New idea, low confidence, worth a quick test.
llm_priority_values.test: Promising — allocate evidence budget to this.
llm_priority_values.retire: Mark for deprecation after current cycle.

inbox_schema.format: JSON array (even for a single op)

prompt_for_tetra: You are analyzing training data for a tetris AI agent. The agent has run 23221 episodes across 6 levels. There are currently 9 active hypotheses guiding its play. Based on the failure patterns, success patterns, and open questions above, please: (1) identify why the agent fails, (2) suggest 2-3 new hypotheses to test using ADD ops, (3) recommend which candidates should be RETIRE'd, (4) optionally MUTATE a weak hypothesis into a stronger variant. Write your response as a valid JSON array of ops to experiments/tetra_inbox.json. See inbox_schema for the exact format.
